cause: Ticks (Rhipicephalus, Ixodes species) attach to skin during walks in grass or wooded areas. They feed on blood and can transmit diseases like Ehrlichiosis and Lyme disease.
warning: If dog shows hind-leg weakness or wobbling after tick bite — rush to vet immediately. This is Tick Paralysis, a medical emergency.

medicines.name: Frontline Plus (Fipronil + S-Methoprene)
medicines.dose: 1 pipette applied to skin between shoulder blades monthly
medicines.note: Do not bathe dog 48 hours before or after application

topical_treatments.name: Himalaya Erina-EP Tick & Flea Shampoo
topical_treatments.use: Lather, leave 5 minutes, rinse. Use weekly during active infestation.

topical_treatments.name: Himalaya Scavon Vet Antiseptic Cream
topical_treatments.use: Apply only if skin becomes irritated or infected after tick removal.

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow Oral Coat Conditioner (Omega-3)
supplements.benefit: Soothes inflamed skin and rebuilds skin barrier damaged by tick bites
supplements.dose: 1 pump on food daily

safety_warnings: Never burn, squeeze or twist a tick — causes it to inject bacteria into the wound
Use fine-tipped tweezers, grasp close to skin, pull upward steadily
Treat ALL pets in the house simultaneously

cause: Ctenocephalides canis fleas jump onto dogs from infested grass, carpets, or other animals. 95% of fleas live in the environment, not on the dog.
warning: A single female flea lays 50 eggs per day — treat the home environment simultaneously or infestation returns within weeks.

medicines.name: Frontline Plus (Fipronil + S-Methoprene)
medicines.dose: Monthly spot-on between shoulder blades
medicines.note: Kills adult fleas AND stops egg development

medicines.name: Comfortis (Spinosad) Oral Tablet
medicines.dose: Once monthly with a full meal
medicines.note: Starts killing fleas within 30 minutes — faster than spot-ons

topical_treatments.name: Himalaya Erina-EP Tick & Flea Shampoo
topical_treatments.use: Lather thoroughly, leave 5 minutes, rinse. Repeat weekly during infestation.

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow (Omega-3 Fish Oil)
supplements.benefit: May reduce skin inflammation associated with flea allergy dermatitis.
supplements.dose: Daily with food during and after infestation

safety_warnings: Wash all dog bedding at 60°C and vacuum all carpets — fleas survive months in fabric
Treat all pets in the house at the same time
Do not use dog flea products on cats — permethrin is fatal to cats

cause: Self-trauma from scratching or licking (triggered by flea bites, allergies, or moisture) creates a warm moist wound that Staphylococcus bacteria rapidly colonize and spread.
warning: Hot spots double in size within hours if licking continues. Use an E-collar immediately.

medicines.name: Veterinary chlorhexidine spray or veterinary hot-spot spray
medicines.dose: Spray thin layer on hot spot 2x daily for up to 7 days
medicines.note: Breaks the itch-scratch cycle. Do not use more than 7 days without vet guidance.

medicines.name: Betamethasone + Gentamicin Spray (Gentabet)
medicines.dose: Spray 2-3 times daily for 5-7 days
medicines.note: For larger or infected hot spots — requires vet prescription

topical_treatments.name: Chlorhexidine 2% Antiseptic Spray
topical_treatments.use: Clip fur around hot spot. Clean with this spray before any cream. Use 2x daily.

topical_treatments.name: Himalaya Scavon Vet Cream
topical_treatments.use: Apply thin layer on cleaned hot spot after antiseptic spray dries

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow (Omega-3)
supplements.benefit: Reduces chronic skin inflammation that triggers hot spots from allergies
supplements.dose: Daily with food as long-term prevention

safety_warnings: Do NOT cover the hot spot — it needs air to dry out and heal
Use E-collar immediately — licking spreads bacteria and doubles size within hours
Hot spot larger than a coin or spreading fast = see vet for oral antibiotics

cause: Bacteria (Staphylococcus, Pseudomonas) or yeast (Malassezia) overgrowth inside the ear canal triggered by trapped moisture, allergies, or ear mites. Common in floppy-eared breeds.
warning: Head tilting to one side + loss of balance = middle ear infection (Otitis Media). Emergency vet visit needed immediately.

medicines.name: Otomax Ear Drops (Gentamicin + Betamethasone + Clotrimazole)
medicines.dose: 4 drops in affected ear 2x daily for 7 days. Massage ear base after each dose.
medicines.note: Covers bacteria AND yeast in one product — most effective for mixed infections

topical_treatments.name: Virbac Epi-Otic Ear Cleanser
topical_treatments.use: Fill ear canal, massage 30 seconds, let dog shake, wipe outer ear with cotton. Use before drops.

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow (Omega-3)
supplements.benefit: Reduces allergy-driven ear inflammation — the #1 root cause of recurring ear infections
supplements.dose: Daily with meals as long-term preventative

safety_warnings: Facial paralysis, severe pain, loss of balance or rapid eye movements require urgent veterinary evaluation.
NEVER insert cotton swabs deep into the ear — can rupture eardrum
Dry ears thoroughly after every bath and swim — moisture is the #1 trigger
Do not use human ear drops unless specifically prescribed by a veterinarian.

cause: Sarcoptes scabiei mites burrow into the outer skin layer causing intense allergic itch. Spreads through direct contact with infected dogs or contaminated bedding.
warning: Sarcoptic mange is contagious to humans. Wear gloves when handling the dog and wash hands thoroughly after contact.

medicines.name: Fluralaner (Bravecto) Chewable Tablet
medicines.dose: Single oral dose by weight with food. One dose = 12 weeks coverage.
medicines.note: Rapidly reduces mite burden and clinical signs. Most effective modern treatment.

medicines.name: Ivermectin Injection
medicines.dose: Given by vet subcutaneously, typically weekly for 4-6 weeks
medicines.note: NEVER use in Collies, Shelties — MDR1 gene causes fatal ivermectin toxicity in these breeds

topical_treatments.name: Himalaya Erina-EP Shampoo
topical_treatments.use: Wear gloves. Lather, leave 10 minutes, rinse. Weekly during treatment to remove mite crusts.

topical_treatments.name: Himalaya Scavon Vet Cream
topical_treatments.use: Apply to raw crusted skin areas after bathing to prevent secondary bacterial infection

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow (Omega-3 + Vitamin E)
supplements.benefit: Rebuilds damaged skin barrier, reduces inflammatory itch, supports coat regrowth
supplements.dose: Daily throughout treatment period and 1 month after recovery

safety_warnings: Zoonotic — contagious to humans. Wear gloves at all times.
Treat ALL pets in the household simultaneously
Wash all bedding in hot water 60°C+ and disinfect living area — mites survive 36 hours off the host
NEVER use ivermectin in Collies, Shelties, Australian Shepherds

cause: Microsporum canis fungus infects hair follicles, causing the characteristic expanding circular patches. Spreads through contact with infected animals, soil, or shared grooming tools.
warning: Ringworm is highly contagious to humans, especially children. Wear gloves when handling the dog.

medicines.name: Itraconazole (Itrafungol / Sporanox)
medicines.dose: 5 mg/kg once daily with food for 3 weeks minimum
medicines.note: Preferred over Griseofulvin. Safer in puppies. Takes 6-8 weeks minimum for full clearance.

topical_treatments.name: Clotrimazole Cream 1% (Canesten)
topical_treatments.use: Apply to patch and 1 cm beyond the ring border twice daily for 4-6 weeks

supplements.name: Biotin (Vitamin B7) Supplement
supplements.benefit: May support coat regrowth during recovery.

safety_warnings: Diagnosis should ideally be confirmed with fungal culture, PCR, or Wood's lamp examination.
Highly contagious to humans — wear gloves, wash hands after contact
Disinfect all brushes, bedding, collars with 1:10 bleach solution — spores survive months
Continue treatment 2 weeks AFTER lesions appear healed to prevent relapse
Isolate the dog from children and other pets during treatment

cause: Genetic overreaction to environmental allergens (pollen, dust mites, mold) or food proteins (chicken, beef, wheat). The skin barrier is defective allowing allergens to penetrate.
warning: Apoquel must not be used in dogs under 12 months or those with serious infections. Consult vet before starting any immunosuppressive therapy.

medicines.name: Oclacitinib (Apoquel) Tablet
medicines.dose: 0.4-0.6 mg/kg twice daily for 14 days, then once daily maintenance
medicines.note: Works within 4 hours. Gold standard for atopic dermatitis. Prescription required.

medicines.name: Cytopoint (Lokivetmab) Injection
medicines.dose: Single injection by vet every 4-8 weeks
medicines.note: Biologic therapy, very safe, lasts up to 8 weeks

topical_treatments.name: Virbac Allermyl Shampoo
topical_treatments.use: Leave on 10 minutes. 2x weekly during flares, once weekly for maintenance.

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow High-dose Omega-3 (EPA + DHA)
supplements.benefit: Reduces skin inflammation at cellular level and rebuilds skin barrier — may improve overall skin health and reduce inflammation.
supplements.dose: 180 mg EPA + 120 mg DHA per 5 kg body weight daily. Results in 4-6 weeks.

safety_warnings: NEVER use human antihistamines like Benadryl without vet dosing guidance
Food allergy trials must use strict hydrolyzed or novel protein diet for 12 full weeks to be valid
Apoquel should not be used in dogs under 12 months

cause: Canine Parvovirus 2 (CPV-2) destroys intestinal lining and bone marrow. Spread through infected feces. Survives in soil for years. Most fatal in unvaccinated puppies 6 weeks to 6 months old.
warning: THIS IS A LIFE-THREATENING EMERGENCY. Go to a vet immediately — every hour matters. Mortality without treatment is up to 91%. There is NO effective home treatment.

medicines.name: IV Fluid Therapy (Normal Saline / Ringer's Lactate)
medicines.dose: Continuous IV drip calculated by vet based on dehydration level
medicines.note: The single most important treatment. Cannot be replaced by oral fluids — the gut is destroyed and cannot absorb.

medicines.name: Maropitant (Cerenia) Anti-nausea
medicines.dose: 1 mg/kg IV or SC once daily at vet clinic
medicines.note: Controls vomiting and allows the gut to rest and begin healing

medicines.name: Ampicillin IV (Antibiotic — prevents sepsis)
medicines.dose: 22 mg/kg IV every 6-8 hours in hospital
medicines.note: Prevents secondary bacterial sepsis while immune system is suppressed

supplements.name: Purina Pro Plan Fortiflora Probiotic
supplements.benefit: Restores gut microbiome destroyed by Parvovirus. ONLY start AFTER vomiting stops and dog can eat.
supplements.dose: 1 sachet sprinkled on food daily for 4-6 weeks post-discharge

safety_warnings: EMERGENCY — rush to vet immediately. Do not wait.
Do NOT attempt oral fluids at home — vomiting gut cannot absorb them and you lose critical time
Vaccinate puppies at 6, 8, 12, and 16 weeks — this disease is entirely preventable
Parvovirus survives in soil 7 years — disinfect all surfaces with 1:30 bleach solution

cause: Bordetella bronchiseptica bacteria alone or combined with canine parainfluenza virus. Spreads through the air in enclosed spaces (kennels, dog parks, grooming salons).
warning: If dog develops fever, stops eating, or cough lasts more than 3 weeks — see vet immediately. May have progressed to pneumonia.

topical_treatments.name: Warm Steam Inhalation (Home Therapy)
topical_treatments.use: Run hot shower, close bathroom door, sit with dog in steam for 10 minutes twice daily. Do NOT direct steam nozzle at dog.

supplements.name: Manuka Honey (Natural Throat Soother)
supplements.benefit: Soothes throat lining, mild antibacterial, reduces cough irritation.Do NOT give to diabetic dogs.

safety_warnings: Isolate from other dogs for 2 weeks — kennel cough is airborne and extremely contagious
Use a harness instead of neck collar — collar pressure on trachea worsens coughing
Vaccinate annually with Bordetella vaccine for dogs that visit kennels or dog parks

cause: Dietary indiscretion (eating grass, garbage, sudden diet change), bacterial/viral infection, intestinal parasites, stress, or food intolerance.
warning: See vet immediately if: blood in vomit or stool, dog is a puppy, symptoms last more than 48 hours, or dog is severely lethargic.

medicines.name: Kaolin-Pectin OTC
medicines.dose: 1-2 ml/kg every 4-6 hours
medicines.note: Coats gut lining, absorbs toxins, reduces fluid loss from diarrhea

supplements.name: Purina Fortiflora Probiotic
supplements.benefit: Restores healthy gut bacteria. Shortens diarrhea episodes by up to 50%.
supplements.dose: 1 sachet sprinkled on food daily. Start as soon as vomiting stops.

supplements.name: ORS / Electral Oral Rehydration Salts
supplements.benefit: Replaces sodium, potassium, glucose lost through vomiting and diarrhea. Prevents dangerous dehydration.
supplements.dose: Use veterinary oral rehydration products when available and under veterinary guidance. Do NOT force-feed to actively vomiting dogs.

safety_warnings: Short fasting may be recommended in adult dogs, but early feeding of a bland diet is often preferred.
NEVER give Imodium (loperamide) to Collies or Shelties — causes fatal neurological toxicity
NEVER give human anti-diarrheal tablets containing bismuth subsalicylate (Pepto-Bismol) — Should not be given unless specifically advised by a veterinarian.
Blood in vomit or stool = emergency vet visit

cause: Roundworms from contaminated soil or mother's milk. Tapeworms from ingesting infected fleas. Hookworms penetrate through skin or ingestion. Puppies commonly infected from mother.
warning: Roundworms can infect humans (Toxocariasis). Hookworm larvae can penetrate human skin. Wash hands thoroughly after handling dog feces.

medicines.name: Fenbendazole (Panacur)
medicines.dose: 50 mg/kg once daily for 3 consecutive days. Repeat in 2-3 weeks.
medicines.note: Covers roundworms, hookworms, whipworms, Giardia.Generally considered safe in puppies and commonly used during pregnancy under veterinary supervision.

medicines.name: Praziquantel + Pyrantel (Drontal Plus)
medicines.dose: Single dose by weight. Repeat in 2-3 weeks.
medicines.note: Best single-tablet option covering tapeworms AND roundworms/hookworms together

supplements.name: Purina Fortiflora Probiotic
supplements.benefit: Restores gut bacteria disrupted by worm damage and deworming medication
supplements.dose: Daily with food for 2-4 weeks after deworming

safety_warnings: Adult dogs should follow a veterinarian-recommended parasite prevention schedule based on lifestyle and regional parasite risk.
Treat fleas to prevent tapeworm re-infection — fleas carry tapeworm larvae
Dispose of dog feces immediately to prevent soil contamination and re-infection
Pale gums + extreme weakness = heavy hookworm burden causing anemia — emergency vet

cause: Common causes include allergies, irritation, dry eye (KCS), foreign bodies, eyelid abnormalities, and secondary bacterial infection.
warning: Cloudy eye, sudden vision loss, or bulging eye = emergency vet immediately. May be Glaucoma or Uveitis.

medicines.name: Tobramycin Eye Drops (Tobrex)
medicines.note: Veterinary eye examination should be performed before starting antibiotic eye medications.

topical_treatments.name: Sterile Saline Eye Flush 0.9%
topical_treatments.use: Tilt dog's head, gently flush eye 2-3 times daily. Wipe clean with soft cotton. Use before eye drops.

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow (Omega-3)
supplements.benefit: Reduces ocular inflammation and supports tear film stability in allergic conjunctivitis
supplements.dose: Daily with food

safety_warnings: Do not use human medicated eye drops unless directed by a veterinarian.
Use a separate cotton ball for each eye to prevent spreading infection
If no improvement in 3 days with OTC care — see vet for prescription drops

cause: Genetic malformation of the hip joint causing laxity and progressive arthritis. Worsened by rapid growth in puppies, obesity, and high-impact exercise during development.
warning: NEVER give human NSAIDs (ibuprofen, naproxen, paracetamol) — they cause fatal gastrointestinal ulcers and kidney failure in dogs.

medicines.name: Meloxicam (Metacam) NSAID
medicines.dose: 0.1 mg/kg once daily with food. Never exceed dose.
medicines.note: Most commonly prescribed dog NSAID. Monitor kidney function with long-term use.

medicines.name: Gabapentin
medicines.dose: 5-10 mg/kg twice daily. Often combined with Meloxicam.
medicines.note: Addresses the nerve pain component of chronic hip dysplasia

supplements.name: Glucosamine + Chondroitin + MSM (Cosequin DS)
supplements.benefit: May support joint health and improve comfort in some dogs.
supplements.dose: Double loading dose for first 4-6 weeks, then maintenance. Takes 4-8 weeks to see results.

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow High-Dose Omega-3
supplements.benefit: EPA reduces inflammatory prostaglandins destroying the joint. Studies show 40% reduction in joint pain with high-dose Omega-3.
supplements.dose: High dose — discuss with vet for correct dose by weight

safety_warnings: NEVER give ibuprofen, aspirin, or paracetamol — Human pain medications can cause severe toxicity and should never be given unless specifically prescribed by a veterinarian.
Maintain lean body weight — every extra kg puts 3-4 kg force on already damaged joints
Avoid high-impact activities — opt for swimming and leash walks on soft surfaces

cause: Normal wear and tear with age causing cartilage breakdown. Worsened by previous injuries, obesity, or breed predisposition (Labs, Goldens, German Shepherds).
warning: Get an orthopedic memory foam bed — hard floors significantly worsen arthritis pain. Install ramps instead of letting the dog jump.

medicines.name: Carprofen (Rimadyl) NSAID
medicines.note: Common first-line NSAID for canine osteoarthritis.Monitor liver enzymes every 6 months with long-term use.

topical_treatments.name: Warm Compress / Heating Pad (Home Therapy)
topical_treatments.use: Apply warm damp cloth to stiff joints for 10-15 minutes twice daily, especially in cold weather. Do NOT use hot — test on your wrist first.

supplements.name: Cosequin DS (Glucosamine + Chondroitin + MSM)
supplements.benefit: Most studied joint supplement. Slows cartilage destruction, lubricates joints, reduces pain. Safe for indefinite long-term use.
supplements.dose: Double dose for first 4-6 weeks (loading phase). Allow 6-8 weeks for full effects.

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow Omega-3 (High Dose EPA+DHA)
supplements.benefit: Reduces inflammatory mediators in joint fluid. Studies show Omega-3 May improve mobility and reduce inflammation.
supplements.dose: High dose by weight — discuss with vet

safety_warnings: NEVER give ibuprofen, aspirin, or paracetamol — cause kidney failure and death in dogs
Sudden inability to stand, severe pain, or rapid worsening of lameness requires veterinary evaluation.
Use ramps for furniture access — jumping causes acute joint injury on top of chronic arthritis

cause: E. coli (most common) bacteria ascending from the urethra into the bladder. More common in females due to shorter urethra. Worsened by obesity, bladder stones, or diabetes.
warning: If dog CANNOT urinate at all despite straining = BLOCKED URETHRA. Life-threatening emergency — go to vet immediately.

medicines.name: Amoxicillin-Clavulanate (Synulox / Clavamox)
medicines.note: Antibiotic selection should ideally be based on urine culture and sensitivity testing. Always complete the full course even if symptoms resolve.

supplements.name: Cranberry Extract (PAC — Proanthocyanidins)
supplements.benefit: Prevents E. coli bacteria from adhering to bladder wall.May help reduce recurrence in some dogs.
supplements.dose: Daily for prevention. Not a substitute for antibiotics in active infection.

supplements.name: D-Mannose Powder
supplements.benefit: Natural sugar that coats E. coli bacteria preventing bladder wall attachment. Works at the infection site directly in urine.
supplements.dose: Mix into drinking water daily. Safe long-term. Combine with cranberry for best effect.

safety_warnings: Complete inability to urinate = EMERGENCY — blocked urethra is fatal within hours without treatment
Recurrent UTIs (3+ per year) need investigation for bladder stones or diabetes
Always provide fresh water — diluted urine flushes the bladder and prevents bacterial growth

cause: Plaque and tartar buildup from lack of dental hygiene. Bacteria form a biofilm that inflames gums, destroys ligaments, and eventually infects the jaw bone.
warning: Periodontal disease may contribute to systemic inflammation and has been associated with effects on internal organs.

topical_treatments.name: Virbac C.E.T. Enzymatic Dog Toothpaste
topical_treatments.use: Brush daily or minimum 3x per week. NEVER use human toothpaste — xylitol is toxic to dogs.

topical_treatments.name: Tropiclean Fresh Breath Water Additive
topical_treatments.use: Add 1 capful per 250ml of water daily. Odorless and tasteless — dogs accept easily.

safety_warnings: NEVER use human toothpaste — fluoride and xylitol are toxic to dogs
Professional dental cleaning under anesthesia is the ONLY way to remove subgingival tartar
Dogs over 3 years have 80% chance of periodontal disease — start dental care as a puppy

cause: High-fat meal, dietary indiscretion (garbage, table scraps), certain medications (steroids), or trauma triggers digestive enzyme leakage that damages surrounding pancreatic tissue.
warning: Prayer position (front down, rear up) + vomiting + abdominal pain = potential severe pancreatitis. Emergency vet immediately.

medicines.name: Maropitant (Cerenia) + IV Fluids
medicines.note: Controls vomiting and allows the pancreas to rest.Small amounts of low-fat food are often reintroduced as soon as vomiting is controlled.

safety_warnings: Pancreatitis can be FATAL in severe cases — do not treat at home
Once a dog has had pancreatitis, permanent low-fat diet (under 10% fat DM) is mandatory
NEVER feed table scraps, bones, or fatty foods to a dog with history of pancreatitis

cause: Destruction of insulin-producing beta cells (Type 1 — most common in dogs) or insulin resistance. More common in intact females and middle-aged to senior dogs.
warning: NEVER skip insulin injections — leads to diabetic ketoacidosis (DKA), a life-threatening emergency. Watch for hypoglycemia: trembling, weakness, collapse — give corn syrup on gums and rush to vet.

medicines.name: Caninsulin (Porcine Insulin) 40 IU/ml
medicines.dose: Injected subcutaneously twice daily 12 hours apart. ALWAYS given after meals.
medicines.note: Starting dose set by vet. Requires glucose curve monitoring. Never skip meals after giving insulin.

safety_warnings: Do not force food or water into an unconscious dog.
Alpha Lipoic Acid (ALA) supplements are TOXIC to dogs — cause hypoglycemia and liver failure. Never give.
Intact female dogs have 3x diabetes risk — neutering often improves insulin sensitivity
Consistent meal times and diet are critical — any change can destabilize blood glucose

cause: Aging (most common), previous kidney infections, long-term NSAID use, dehydration, leptospirosis, certain toxin ingestion, or immune-mediated disease.
warning: NEVER give NSAIDs (Meloxicam, Carprofen) to a dog with kidney disease — they reduce blood flow to kidneys and cause acute kidney failure.

medicines.name: Benazepril (ACE Inhibitor)
medicines.dose: 0.25-0.5 mg/kg once daily
medicines.note: Reduces pressure in kidney blood vessels, slowing progression of CKD

medicines.name: Maropitant (Cerenia) Anti-nausea
medicines.dose: 1 mg/kg once daily
medicines.note: Controls uremic nausea and significantly improves appetite and quality of life

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow Omega-3 (EPA+DHA — Renoprotective)
supplements.benefit: Reduces glomerular inflammation. Clinical studies show it slows CKD progression and improves survival time.

supplements.name: Aluminum Hydroxide (Phosphate Binder)
supplements.benefit: Binds dietary phosphorus in the gut before absorption. Reducing phosphorus is the most important dietary intervention in CKD.
supplements.dose: Titrated by vet based on blood phosphorus levels. Mix into food.

safety_warnings: Renal prescription diet is NOT optional — it is the most critical intervention after diagnosis
NEVER give NSAIDs to a dog with kidney disease — can trigger acute-on-chronic kidney failure
Home subcutaneous fluid therapy taught by vet can add months to years of quality life

cause: Toxin ingestion (xylitol, mushrooms, medications), infectious hepatitis, chronic copper accumulation (Bedlingtons, Dalmatians), portosystemic shunt, or long-term steroid use.
warning: ANY yellow color in eyes or skin = medical emergency. Head pressing against walls = hepatic encephalopathy — emergency vet immediately.

medicines.name: Ursodeoxycholic Acid (UDCA / Ursodiol)
medicines.dose: 10-15 mg/kg once daily with food
medicines.note: Improves bile flow, reduces inflammation, protects liver cells. Safe for long-term use.

supplements.name: SAMe (S-Adenosyl Methionine) — Denosyl SD4
supplements.benefit: Most evidence-based liver supplement for dogs. Replenishes glutathione (liver's primary antioxidant), directly protecting liver cells.
supplements.dose: Give on empty stomach 1-2 hours before food. Do NOT crush the enteric coating.

safety_warnings: Yellow eyes or skin = EMERGENCY — see vet immediately
Head pressing against walls = hepatic encephalopathy = EMERGENCY
NEVER give Tylenol/paracetamol/acetaminophen to dogs — even one tablet causes fatal liver failure

cause: Lack of independence training in puppyhood, history of abandonment, trauma, or genetic predisposition to fear. Storm phobia often worsens with age.
warning: Punishment during anxiety attacks makes anxiety dramatically worse. Never scold an anxious dog.

medicines.name: Fluoxetine (Reconcile) SSRI
medicines.note: Must be combined with behavioral modification to be effective. Not a standalone fix.

medicines.name: Trazodone (Situational anxiety)
medicines.note: Works within 1-2 hours. Good for storms, fireworks, vet visits.

topical_treatments.name: Adaptil DAP Diffuser + Collar
topical_treatments.use: Plug diffuser into main room. Replace refill monthly. Collar worn continuously.

supplements.name: L-Theanine + Melatonin Calming Chew (Zesty Paws Calming Bites)
supplements.benefit: L-Theanine raises GABA and serotonin producing calm without sedation.May help some dogs with situational anxiety and sleep regulation.

safety_warnings: NEVER use human CBD products — many contain xylitol (toxic) or untested concentrations
Punishment worsens anxiety — never scold or punish an anxious dog

cause: Staphylococcus pseudintermedius overgrowth following skin trauma, moisture, allergies (most common root cause), hormonal imbalance, or immune suppression.
warning: Recurrent pyoderma almost always has an underlying cause (allergies, hypothyroidism, Cushing's). Treating only the bacteria without addressing root cause leads to relapse.

medicines.name: Amoxicillin-Clavulanate (Clavamox / Synulox)
medicines.note: Complete the FULL course — stopping early causes antibiotic-resistant bacteria (MRSP)

topical_treatments.name: Virbac Pyohex Shampoo (Chlorhexidine 3% + Miconazole 1%)
topical_treatments.use: Leave on 5-10 minutes before rinsing. Use twice weekly throughout antibiotic course.

topical_treatments.name: Chlorhexidine 2% Spray
topical_treatments.use: Spray on affected areas, allow to dry. Use daily between shampoo days.

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow (Omega-3 — Skin Barrier Repair)
supplements.benefit: May support skin barrier health and reduce inflammation. Reduces allergy-driven inflammation triggering recurrent pyoderma.
supplements.dose: Daily with food

safety_warnings: Never stop antibiotics early even if skin looks better — biofilms need the full course to eradicate
MRSP (Methicillin-Resistant Staph) is emerging in dogs — culture before treating recurrent non-responding cases
Recurrent pyoderma without clear cause needs full hormonal and allergy workup

cause: Idiopathic epilepsy (genetic — Beagles, Labs, German Shepherds most common). Secondary epilepsy from brain tumor, head trauma, toxin ingestion, hypoglycemia, or liver disease.
warning: Seizure lasting more than 5 minutes OR multiple seizures within 1 hour = STATUS EPILEPTICUS — rush to emergency vet immediately. This is fatal without treatment.

medicines.name: Phenobarbital
medicines.note: Most effective first-line treatment. Requires liver enzyme monitoring every 6 months.

medicines.name: Potassium Bromide (KBr)
medicines.dose: 30-40 mg/kg once daily mixed into food
medicines.note: Added to Phenobarbital for better control. Takes 3-4 months to reach steady-state levels.

supplements.name: MCT Oil (Medium Chain Triglycerides)
supplements.benefit: Provides alternative fuel for neurons, reducing seizure frequency.Some studies suggest MCT-enriched diets may reduce seizure frequency in certain dogs.

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow Omega-3 (DHA — Neuroprotective)
supplements.benefit: DHA is a primary structural component of brain cell membranes. Supports neurological health and may reduce seizure severity.
supplements.dose: Daily with food

safety_warnings: During seizure: stay calm, time it, do NOT put anything in dog's mouth — they cannot swallow their tongue
NEVER stop anticonvulsant medication suddenly — causes severe rebound seizures that can be fatal
5+ minute seizure = emergency vet immediately

aliases: dog not putting weight on leg
cause: Overstretching or tearing of ligaments (sprain) or muscles/tendons (strain) from sudden movements, jumping, or rough play. Common in active and sporting breeds.
warning: NEVER give human painkillers (ibuprofen, paracetamol, aspirin, Crocin) — all are toxic and potentially fatal to dogs. Only use dog-specific NSAIDs prescribed by a vet.

medicines.name: Meloxicam (Metacam) — Dog-safe NSAID only
medicines.note: One commonly prescribed canine NSAID. Never give ibuprofen, aspirin, or paracetamol — Human pain medications can cause severe toxicity and should never be given without veterinary guidance.

topical_treatments.name: Cold Pack Therapy (First 24-48 hours)
topical_treatments.use: Wrap ice pack in towel, apply to swollen area 10-15 minutes 3x daily for first 48 hours. Never apply ice directly to skin.

topical_treatments.name: Warm Compress (After 48 hours)
topical_treatments.use: After first 48 hours, switch to warm damp cloth for 10-15 minutes 2x daily to improve circulation and healing.

supplements.name: Glucosamine + Chondroitin (Cosequin DS)
supplements.benefit: May support joint health during recovery.. Reduces secondary joint inflammation.
supplements.dose: Double loading dose for first 4 weeks during recovery

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow Omega-3
supplements.benefit: Reduces acute joint and soft tissue inflammation during injury recovery
supplements.dose: Daily with food during recovery period

safety_warnings: NEVER give ibuprofen, aspirin, paracetamol, or Crocin — ALL are toxic to dogs and can cause fatal kidney failure
Strict rest for minimum 2 weeks — no running, jumping, or stairs
If dog is completely non-weight-bearing for more than 24 hours — see vet immediately to rule out fracture or ligament rupture

cause: Being left in a hot car, overexertion in hot humid weather, lack of shade/water. Brachycephalic breeds (Bulldogs, Pugs, French Bulldogs) cannot pant efficiently and overheat faster.
warning: NEVER use ice or ice water to cool a heatstroke dog — causes blood vessel spasm that traps heat inside the core and can worsen outcome.

medicines.name: IV Normal Saline (Emergency Internal Cooling + Shock)
medicines.note: IV fluids are an important component of hospital treatment.

topical_treatments.name: Tepid Water Cooling Protocol (Emergency First Aid)
topical_treatments.use: Apply room-temperature (NOT cold/ice) water to armpits, groin, paws. Fan continuously. Stop cooling when temperature reaches 39.5°C to prevent rebound hypothermia.

supplements.name: Electral / ORS Oral Rehydration Salts
supplements.benefit: Replaces electrolytes lost through extreme panting and hyperthermia. May help replace fluid and electrolyte losses once the dog is alert and able to swallow safely.
supplements.dose: Offer freely once conscious and swallowing. Do NOT give to unconscious dog. Follow with IV fluids at vet.

safety_warnings: NEVER leave a dog in a parked car even for 5 minutes — temperature reaches fatal levels in under 10 minutes
NEVER use ice or ice water — causes peripheral vasoconstriction trapping heat in core
Frantic panting + bright red gums + collapse = heatstroke emergency. Rush to vet immediately.
Brachycephalic breeds (Bulldogs, Pugs, Frenchies) are at extreme risk even in mild heat

cause: Congenital malformation of the trochlear groove causing the kneecap to slip out of position. Genetic condition — very common in small breeds: Chihuahuas, Pomeranians, Yorkshire Terriers, Toy Poodles, Maltese.
warning: Grade 3-4 patellar luxation causes permanent bone deformity and severe arthritis without surgery. Do not delay surgical consultation.

medicines.name: Meloxicam (Metacam) NSAID
medicines.note: Short-term pain relief during episodes. Not a long-term solution.

supplements.name: Cosequin DS (Glucosamine + Chondroitin)
supplements.benefit: May support joint comfort and mobility and reduces secondary arthritis from repeated luxation events
supplements.dose: Double loading dose for first 4-6 weeks, then maintenance. Use daily continuously.

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow Omega-3
supplements.benefit: Reduces synovial joint inflammation from repeated cartilage trauma during luxation
supplements.dose: Daily with food

safety_warnings: Grade 3-4 luxation needs surgery — permanent arthritis and disability without it
Avoid jumping, stairs, and twisting movements — these worsen cartilage damage
Do not breed dogs with Grade 3-4 patellar luxation — it is an inheritable condition
NEVER give ibuprofen or paracetamol — use only vet-prescribed Meloxicam

aliases: dog non weight bearing hind leg
cause: Rupture of the cranial cruciate ligament from acute trauma (jumping awkwardly) or chronic degeneration over time. Common in Labrador Retrievers, Rottweilers, Staffordshire Bull Terriers, and overweight dogs.
warning: An unstable knee destroys the meniscus cartilage within weeks. Surgery delayed beyond 4-6 weeks causes irreversible joint damage.Untreated instability often accelerates osteoarthritis progression.

medicines.name: Meloxicam (Metacam) NSAID
medicines.note: NEVER give human NSAIDs — ibuprofen and paracetamol are fatal to dogs

topical_treatments.name: Cold Pack Therapy (First 48 hours post-injury)
topical_treatments.use: Wrap ice pack in towel, apply to knee 10-15 minutes 3x daily for first 48 hours only. Reduces acute swelling.

supplements.name: Cosequin DS (Glucosamine + Chondroitin + Hyaluronic Acid)
supplements.benefit: Prevents secondary meniscal and cartilage damage in the unstable joint. Essential before and after surgery.
supplements.dose: Double loading dose immediately on diagnosis. Continue daily indefinitely.

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow High-Dose Omega-3
supplements.benefit: Reduces synovitis (joint lining inflammation) driven by the unstable knee. Slows cartilage breakdown.
supplements.dose: High dose by body weight — discuss with vet

safety_warnings: NEVER give ibuprofen, aspirin, or paracetamol — Human pain medications can cause severe toxicity.
TPLO surgery has 90%+ return to full function — the most successful treatment for large breeds
Strict rest — no running, jumping, or off-leash activity until surgically repaired
Without surgery, the dog will develop severe painful arthritis within months

cause: Disc material herniates and compresses the spinal cord. Genetic predisposition in Chondrodystrophic breeds: Dachshunds, Corgis, Basset Hounds, Beagles, French Bulldogs, Shih Tzus.
warning: ANY hind leg weakness in a Dachshund is an EMERGENCY. Spinal cord death is permanent after 24-48 hours of compression. Do not wait.

medicines.name: Gabapentin + Methocarbamol
medicines.note: Controls severe spinal pain and muscle spasms. Used for conservative management cases.

supplements.name: Methylcobalamin B12 + B-Vitamin Complex
supplements.benefit: May support neurologic recovery. and nerve fiber regeneration after spinal cord compression

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow Omega-3 (DHA — Neuroprotective)
supplements.benefit: DHA is primary fat in myelin (nerve coating).

safety_warnings: Hind leg weakness in Dachshund = EMERGENCY — go to vet immediately, every hour matters
Strict cage rest means NO jumping, stairs, or running — one small room only for 6-8 weeks
Physical rehabilitation after surgery dramatically improves recovery — find a vet rehab specialist
Never lift a dog with IVDD by the torso — support front and back equally

cause: Canine Distemper Virus (CDV) spread through airborne respiratory secretions. Attacks respiratory, gastrointestinal, and nervous systems. Most fatal in unvaccinated puppies and young dogs.
warning: THIS IS A MEDICAL EMERGENCY. Distemper is largely fatal or causes permanent neurological damage without early intensive treatment. Vaccinate with DHPPiL vaccine — entirely preventable.

medicines.name: IV Fluid Therapy + Broad-spectrum Antibiotics
medicines.note: No antiviral cure exists. Treatment is intensive supportive care only.

medicines.name: Phenobarbital (Anti-seizure)
medicines.dose: Per vet protocol for neurological distemper cases
medicines.note: Controls seizures but does not treat the virus. Supportive only.

topical_treatments.name: Sterile Saline Eye and Nose Wipes
topical_treatments.use: Gently wipe discharge from eyes and nose 3-4 times daily with soft cotton dampened in saline. Prevents secondary infection.

supplements.name: Beaphar Multivitamine Liquid (Vitamin A + C + E)
supplements.benefit: May help support nutritional status during recovery.

safety_warnings: EMERGENCY — rush to vet immediately. Do not wait even one hour.
Vaccinate with DHPPiL at 6, 8, 12, and 16 weeks — this disease is entirely preventable
Isolate infected dog completely — CDV spreads through the air
Distemper causes permanent neurological damage (seizures, twitching) even in survivors

cause: Dirofilaria immitis worms transmitted through mosquito bites. Microfilariae develop into adult worms in the heart and pulmonary arteries over 6 months causing progressive cardiovascular damage.
warning: Heartworm testing is recommended before starting prevention in dogs with unknown status.

medicines.name: Doxycycline (Pre-treatment — 4 weeks)
medicines.note: Kills Wolbachia bacteria inside heartworms, weakening them and making treatment safer

medicines.name: Melarsomine (Immiticide) — Vet Only
medicines.note: ABSOLUTE CAGE REST required — exercise causes dead worm pulmonary embolism which is fatal

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow Omega-3 (Cardiac + Pulmonary Support)
supplements.benefit: Reduces pulmonary arterial inflammation from dead worm debris during treatment. Supports heart function.
supplements.dose: Begin after Melarsomine treatment under vet guidance

safety_warnings: Heartworm treatment requires ABSOLUTE CAGE REST — even walking to the yard can cause fatal pulmonary embolism
Prevention (monthly Heartgard/Advocate) is vastly safer and cheaper than treatment
Test dog for heartworm BEFORE starting any preventative medication
Veterinarians in endemic regions often recommend year-round prevention.

cause: Stomach expands with gas and twists on itself, cutting off blood supply. Genetic predisposition in large deep-chested breeds: Great Danes, Standard Poodles, German Shepherds, Weimaraners, Dobermans.
warning: GDV is one of the most life-threatening emergencies in dogs. Every minute without treatment increases mortality. Do not drive slowly — rush to 24-hour emergency vet immediately.

medicines.name: IV Fluid Shock Protocol + Emergency Surgery
medicines.note: Without surgery mortality is nearly 100%. Time is critical.

supplements.name: Purina Fortiflora Probiotic (Post-surgery recovery)
supplements.benefit: Restores gut microbiome after the massive disruption of GDV surgery and antibiotic treatment

safety_warnings: EMERGENCY — rush to 24-hour emergency vet immediately. Do not wait.
Prevention: Feed 2-3 small meals daily instead of 1 large meal. No exercise 2 hours after eating.
Prophylactic gastropexy surgery during neutering recommended for all large deep-chested breeds
Rapidly expanding hard belly + retching with no vomit = GDV until proven otherwise

cause: Immune-mediated destruction of the thyroid gland (lymphocytic thyroiditis) causing slowed metabolism affecting every organ system. Common in Golden Retrievers, Dobermans, Irish Setters, Cocker Spaniels.
warning: Routine iodine supplementation is not recommended unless specifically advised by a veterinarian.

medicines.name: Levothyroxine (Thyrozine / Soloxine) T4
medicines.note: Must be given at the same time each day. Requires T4 blood level monitoring 4-6 hours post-pill every 6 months.

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow Omega-3 (Coat + Skin Recovery)
supplements.benefit: Supports skin and coat recovery from hypothyroid-related hair loss and dry skin while Levothyroxine levels stabilize
supplements.dose: Daily with food

supplements.name: Selenium + Vitamin E (Thyroid Antioxidant)
supplements.benefit: Supplement selenium only if specifically recommended by a veterinarian. Deficiency worsens hypothyroidism.
supplements.dose: Do NOT supplement iodine. Selenium only as directed by vet.

safety_warnings: NEVER supplement with iodine — causes autoimmune thyroid flare and worsens the condition
Levothyroxine must be given on empty stomach — food reduces absorption by up to 40%
Get a thyroid blood test for any middle-aged dog with unexplained weight gain before assuming it is just old age
Most dogs show dramatic improvement within 4-6 weeks of starting treatment

cause: Giardia duodenalis protozoa ingested through contaminated water (streams, puddles, standing water) or infected feces. Common in puppies and dogs in kennels or shelters.
warning: Some Giardia strains may infect humans, but transmission from dogs is considered uncommon. Wash hands thoroughly after handling dog and feces.

medicines.name: Fenbendazole (Panacur)
medicines.note: Excellent alternative or combination with Metronidazole. Safe in puppies and pregnant dogs.

supplements.name: Purina Fortiflora Probiotic (High-Potency Gut Recovery)
supplements.benefit: Giardia severely disrupts gut microbiome. High-dose probiotics restore lactobacillus and bifidobacteria,May help restore normal intestinal microbiota.

safety_warnings: Zoonotic — can spread to humans. Wear gloves when handling dog feces.
Giardia cysts are resistant to most disinfectants — use quaternary ammonium compounds or bleach
Dogs must not swim in shared water sources until 2 negative fecal tests confirmed
Avoid letting dog drink from puddles, streams, or standing water

cause: Leptospira spirochete bacteria transmitted through contact with water or soil contaminated by infected wildlife urine (rats, raccoons, deer). Common near rivers, ponds, puddles, and flood water.
warning: Leptospirosis IS CONTAGIOUS TO HUMANS. Wear gloves when handling a suspected lepto dog. This is a notifiable zoonotic disease.

medicines.name: Ampicillin IV (Acute Phase — Hospital)
medicines.note: Kills spirochetes rapidly. Given IV in hospital during acute severe phase.

medicines.name: Doxycycline (Carrier Phase Clearance)
medicines.note: Critical — clears bacterial carrier state in kidneys to prevent ongoing shedding to humans

supplements.name: SAMe (Denosyl) + Milk Thistle (Liver Recovery)
supplements.benefit: Leptospirosis causes severe liver damage. SAMe replenishes glutathione and May help support liver health during recovery.
supplements.dose: Begin after acute phase stabilizes, under vet guidance

safety_warnings: Zoonotic — CONTAGIOUS TO HUMANS. Wear gloves, wash hands,Follow local veterinary and public health guidance.
Vaccinate with Lepto 4 (4-serovar) vaccine annually especially near water or wildlife areas
NEVER let dogs drink from puddles, ponds, or flood water — primary source of Leptospira
EMERGENCY — severe muscle pain + fever + yellow eyes = rush to vet immediately

cause: Sudden severe immune overreaction to insect stings (bee, wasp), vaccine reactions, certain foods, or medications. Anaphylaxis involves mast cell degranulation releasing massive histamine.
warning: ANY breathing difficulty, pale gums, or collapse after allergic reaction = ANAPHYLAXIS. Rush to emergency vet immediately — minutes matter.

medicines.name: Diphenhydramine (Benadryl) — Mild reactions only
medicines.note: For mild hives or face swelling with NO breathing difficulty. Any breathing issue = vet immediately.

medicines.name: Epinephrine (Adrenaline) — Anaphylaxis
medicines.note: Reverses bronchospasm and cardiovascular collapse. Vet only.

topical_treatments.name: Cold Compress (Sting/bite site relief)
topical_treatments.use: Wrap ice pack in towel, apply to swollen sting/bite area for 10 minutes. Reduces local histamine response and pain.

supplements.name: Quercetin (Natural Mast Cell Stabilizer — Preventative)
supplements.benefit: Inhibits mast cell histamine release. Preventative supplement for dogs with known environmental allergies.May provide supportive allergy management in some dogs.

safety_warnings: Breathing difficulty + pale gums + collapse = ANAPHYLAXIS EMERGENCY. Rush to vet.
NEVER give Benadryl-D or any antihistamine with decongestants — pseudoephedrine causes heart attacks in dogs
Ask vet about emergency epinephrine kit if dog has had previous anaphylactic reactions
Remove bee stinger by scraping sideways — never squeeze it as this injects more venom

cause: Demodex mites normally live harmlessly in hair follicles but overpopulate when a dog's immune system is compromised — young puppies, malnourished dogs, or dogs on immunosuppressants.
warning: Demodectic mange is NOT contagious to humans or other dogs. However, do not breed dogs with generalized Demodectic mange — it may indicate an inherited immune deficiency.

medicines.name: Fluralaner (Bravecto) Chewable Tablet
medicines.dose: Single oral dose by weight every 12 weeks. Usually 2-3 doses needed for complete resolution.
medicines.note: Most effective modern treatment for Demodectic mange. Kills mites inside hair follicles.

topical_treatments.name: Virbac Pyohex Shampoo (Chlorhexidine 3% + Miconazole 1%)
topical_treatments.use: Treats secondary bacterial and fungal infections in Demodex-damaged follicles. Leave on 5-10 minutes, rinse. Use twice weekly.

supplements.name: Beaphar Multivitamine (Immune Booster — Vitamin C + Zinc)
supplements.benefit: Provides nutritional support during recovery. Speeds coat regrowth.
supplements.dose: Daily with food. Immune support is critical — Demodex only explodes when immunity is weak.

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow Omega-3 (Skin + Immunity)
supplements.benefit: Rebuilds damaged skin barrier in affected areas and supports immune regulation
supplements.dose: Daily with food throughout treatment

safety_warnings: NOT contagious to humans or other dogs — do not isolate unnecessarily
Localized cases (small patches) in puppies often self-resolve as immune system matures
Do not breed dogs with generalized Demodectic mange — may indicate inherited immune deficiency
Secondary bacterial skin infections are common — treat with antibacterial shampoo simultaneously

cause: Caloric intake exceeding energy expenditure. Overfeeding, excessive treats, inadequate exercise, neutering (lowers metabolic rate 20-30%), hypothyroidism, or Cushing's disease.
warning: Obese dogs live 2.5 years less on average than lean dogs. Obesity is the #1 preventable health issue in dogs.

supplements.name: L-Carnitine (Fat Metabolism Support)
supplements.benefit: Transports fatty acids into mitochondria for burning as energy. Preserves lean muscle mass during weight loss.

supplements.name: Psyllium Husk Fiber Supplement
supplements.benefit: Creates feeling of fullness, slows digestion, regulates blood sugar. Helps dog feel satisfied on reduced calories.

safety_warnings: Never crash-diet a dog — rapid weight loss causes hepatic lipidosis (fatty liver disease)
Target 1-2% body weight loss per week — no faster
Rule out hypothyroidism or Cushing's with blood tests if weight gain is sudden or unexplained
Measure food precisely with a scale — no free feeding or guessing portions

cause: Ingrown hair, debris embedded between toes, Staphylococcal bacterial infection, Demodex mites, allergy-driven skin inflammation, or foreign body (grass awn). Short-coated breeds most affected: Bulldogs, Labs, Boxers.
warning: NEVER squeeze or lance an interdigital cyst at home — this drives infection deeper into tissue and causes severe pain and potential abscess.

medicines.name: Cephalexin (Ceporex)
medicines.note: Deep folliculitis requires long antibiotic courses — stopping early causes recurrence

topical_treatments.name: Betadine (Povidone-Iodine 1%) Foot Soak
topical_treatments.active: Povidone-Iodine diluted to weak tea color
topical_treatments.use: Soak affected paw 5-10 minutes twice daily. Pat dry thoroughly after. Kills bacteria and softens infected follicle.

topical_treatments.name: Epsom Salt (Magnesium Sulfate) Foot Soak
topical_treatments.use: 2 tbsp Epsom salt in 2 cups warm water. Soak paw 10 minutes twice daily. Reduces swelling and draws out infection.

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow Omega-3 (Allergy + Skin Barrier)
supplements.benefit: Addresses allergic skin inflammation — the #1 root cause of recurrent interdigital cysts in predisposed breeds
supplements.dose: Daily with food long-term

safety_warnings: NEVER squeeze or lance the cyst at home — drives infection deeper
Use E-collar to prevent licking — moisture from licking prevents healing
Check for embedded grass awns (foxtails) — these migrate deep and require surgery
Dry paws thoroughly after every walk — moisture promotes bacterial growth

cause: Insufficient fiber in diet leading to soft stools that don't naturally express the glands. Obesity, narrow anal sac ducts, food allergies, and poor muscle tone also contribute.
warning: Do NOT attempt home anal gland expression without proper training — incorrect technique ruptures the gland or pushes infection deeper causing abscess.

medicines.name: Amoxicillin-Clavulanate (if anal sac abscess develops)
medicines.dose: 12.5 mg/kg twice daily for 7-10 days
medicines.note: For infected or abscessed anal sacs only. Requires manual drainage or flushing by vet first.

topical_treatments.name: Warm Saline Compress
topical_treatments.use: Apply warm damp cloth to the anal area for 5-10 minutes twice daily. Reduces pain and inflammation, may encourage natural drainage.

supplements.name: Psyllium Husk Fiber Supplement
supplements.benefit: Increases stool bulk and firmness. Firmer stools naturally express anal glands with each bowel movement, preventing impaction.

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow Omega-3 (Anti-inflammatory for ducts)
supplements.benefit: May support overall skin and inflammatory health.
supplements.dose: Daily with food

safety_warnings: Never express anal glands at home without training — can rupture the gland
Recurrent impaction needs vet workup for allergies or diet issues — not just repeated expressions
An anal sac abscess will rupture if untreated — see vet before this happens

cause: Secondary to kennel cough or dog flu, aspiration of food/vomit (aspiration pneumonia), bacterial infection (Bordetella, Streptococcus), or fungal infection. Compromises oxygen exchange in the lungs.
warning: Blue or purple gums = oxygen deprivation EMERGENCY. Go to vet immediately — do not wait.

topical_treatments.name: Saline Nebulization + Coupage
topical_treatments.use: Inhaled saline loosens thick mucus. Coupage (rhythmic chest clapping) mobilizes secretions for coughing out. Done by vet nurses in hospital. Can continue at home with human nebulizer 3-4x daily.

supplements.name: N-Acetylcysteine (NAC) — Mucolytic
supplements.benefit: Directly breaks bonds in lung mucus,May help loosen respiratory secretions. Makes coughing more effective and speeds clearance of infected material.
supplements.dose: Oral during home recovery phase under vet guidance

safety_warnings: Blue or purple gums = EMERGENCY — go to vet immediately
Strict rest for minimum 3-4 weeks even after symptoms resolve — lungs need time to heal
Aspiration pneumonia risk is highest in vomiting dogs, those under sedation, or with megaesophagus

cause: Idiopathic vestibular syndrome (most common — unknown cause, resolves on its own), inner ear infection, hypothyroidism, or rarely brain tumor. Very alarming appearance but often not serious.
warning: Idiopathic vestibular disease looks like a stroke but usually RESOLVES on its own. Most dogs improve significantly within 72 hours. See vet to rule out ear infection and brain tumor.

medicines.name: Maropitant (Cerenia) Anti-nausea
medicines.note: Controls profound nausea and vomiting from vestibular-induced vertigo. Significantly improves quality of life.

medicines.name: Meclizine (OTC Motion Sickness Tablet)
medicines.note: Reduces spinning sensation from vestibular dysfunction. Mild sedative effect also helps.

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow Omega-3 + B-Vitamin Complex
supplements.benefit: DHA in Omega-3 supports myelin repair in the vestibular nerve. B vitamins (B6, B12) support nerve function recovery.
supplements.dose: Begin from day 1 of recovery. Continue for 4-8 weeks.

safety_warnings: Prevent falls — create a padded area and assist walking. Keep away from stairs and elevated surfaces.
See vet to rule out ear infection, hypothyroidism, and brain tumor before assuming idiopathic
If no improvement at 72 hours — see vet immediately as brain tumor becomes more likely
Do not leave dog alone near water sources during vestibular episode

cause: Canine influenza virus H3N2 (predominant) or H3N8. Spread through respiratory droplets and contaminated surfaces. Can sweep through kennels and dog parks rapidly with presymptomatic shedding.
warning: Isolate the infected dog completely from other dogs — dog flu spreads before symptoms appear. Seek vet if labored breathing develops — pneumonia can develop rapidly.

medicines.name: Doxycycline (Secondary bacterial pneumonia prevention)
medicines.note: For secondary bacterial bronchopneumonia which is the main cause of mortality in dog flu

topical_treatments.name: Cool Mist Humidifier
topical_treatments.use: Run in dog's resting area throughout recovery. Moisturizes inflamed airways, loosens secretions, reduces coughing. Keep water tank clean to prevent mold.

supplements.name: Zesty Paws Immune Bites (Elderberry + Vitamin C + Zinc)
supplements.benefit: May provide nutritional antioxidant support. Vitamin C + Zinc boost natural killer cell activity. Reduces viral duration and severity.
supplements.dose: Safe during infection. Do not exceed recommended dose of Zinc — excess is toxic to dogs.

safety_warnings: Isolate completely — dog flu spreads before symptoms appear (presymptomatic shedding)
Vaccinate social dogs annually especially those visiting kennels, dog parks, or groomers
Seek vet if fever exceeds 40°C or dog develops labored breathing — pneumonia risk is high
Disinfect all shared surfaces, bowls, and bedding with quaternary ammonium disinfectant

aliases: dog hair loss trunk muscle wasting
cause: Pituitary gland tumor (85% of cases) stimulating excess ACTH causing adrenal glands to overproduce cortisol. Or adrenal tumor directly. Iatrogenic Cushing's from long-term steroid use.
warning: Any dose adjustments or discontinuation should be directed by a veterinarian. Dogs on long-term steroids must also never stop suddenly.

medicines.name: Trilostane (Vetoryl)
medicines.note: Gold-standard treatment. Never stop suddenly — taper under vet supervision only.

supplements.name: Melatonin (Adrenal Function Modulator)
supplements.benefit: At specific doses, melatonin inhibits adrenal steroid production and can reduce cortisol. Useful adjunct in mild cases.

supplements.name: Flaxseed Hull Lignans
supplements.benefit: Plant lignans inhibit an enzyme involved in adrenal steroid production. Shown to reduce cortisol. Often combined with Melatonin.

safety_warnings: NEVER abruptly stop Trilostane — causes Addisonian crisis (life-threatening adrenal failure)
Watch for Addison's disease signs on Trilostane: sudden weakness, vomiting, collapse
Dogs on long-term steroids (more than 6 weeks) must never stop steroids suddenly
Recurring skin infections + pot belly + extreme thirst = strong Cushing's suspicion — blood test needed

cause: Dogs chew and swallow non-food objects — especially puppies. High-risk items: corn cobs, socks, toys, bones, rubber balls, bottle caps, fruit pits, hair ties.
warning: DO NOT give hydrogen peroxide at home to induce vomiting — causes severe hemorrhagic gastroenteritis in dogs. Only a vet can safely induce vomiting.

medicines.name: Apomorphine (Induced Vomiting) — Vet Only
medicines.note: NEVER induce vomiting at home for sharp objects, caustic chemicals, or ingestion more than 2 hours ago.

supplements.name: Purina Fortiflora
supplements.benefit: Restores gut microbiome and digestive function after intestinal surgery and antibiotic treatment
supplements.dose: Begin when eating resumes post-surgery. Use for minimum 4-6 weeks.

safety_warnings: Do not induce vomiting at home unless specifically instructed by a veterinarian.
DO NOT wait and see if symptoms are present — intestinal perforation is fatal within 24 hours
Keep socks, children's toys, corn cobs, and fruit pits out of dog reach at all times
No bowel movement in 24 hours + vomiting + lethargy = possible obstruction. Emergency vet.

cause: Scratch to the cornea from a cat claw, thorn, grass awn, or from rubbing. Brachycephalic breeds (Bulldogs, Pugs, French Bulldogs) have protruding eyes highly prone to corneal ulcers.
warning: A corneal ulcer can perforate the eye within 24-48 hours if untreated. This causes permanent vision loss or loss of the eye. Do not delay vet visit.
medicines: Medication choice depends on ulcer depth, infection status, and ophthalmic examination.

topical_treatments.name: Sterile Saline Eye Flush 0.9%
topical_treatments.use: Gently flush eye to remove debris before applying drops. Use before every drop application.

supplements.name: Vitamin C + Omega-3 (Corneal Healing Support)
supplements.benefit: Vitamin C is essential for collagen synthesis in corneal repair. Omega-3 reduces ocular inflammation during healing.
supplements.dose: 125 mg Vitamin C per 5 kg daily during healing period

safety_warnings: NEVER use steroid eye drops on a corneal ulcer — causes perforation and permanent blindness
NEVER use human Visine or any OTC eye drops not specifically for corneal ulcers
Use E-collar immediately to prevent rubbing — rubbing converts a simple ulcer into a perforated one
Brachycephalic breeds need immediate vet attention — their ulcers progress faster than other breeds

cause: Abnormal proliferation of mast cells. Strong genetic predisposition in Boxers, Bulldogs, Golden Retrievers, Labrador Retrievers, Pugs, and Boston Terriers. Can develop at any age.
warning: ALL new skin lumps should be tested with fine-needle aspirate (FNA). NEVER squeeze or massage a skin lump — if it is a mast cell tumor, this causes massive histamine release and anaphylaxis.

medicines.name: Diphenhydramine (Benadryl) — Pre-surgical antihistamine
medicines.dispensing: OTC at surgical dose per vet
medicines.note: Prevents histamine crisis (anaphylaxis) when mast cells are disrupted during surgery or examination

medicines.name: Toceranib Phosphate (Palladia) — Targeted Therapy
medicines.dose: Per oncology protocol. Every other day dosing.
medicines.note: For Grade 2-3 or recurrent MCTs with c-KIT mutation. 40-60% response rate.

supplements.name: Quercetin (Natural Mast Cell Stabilizer)
supplements.benefit: Inhibits mast cell degranulation,May provide supportive anti-inflammatory effects. Synergistic with antihistamine treatment.

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow Omega-3 EPA+DHA (Anti-tumor Inflammation)
supplements.benefit: EPA inhibits pro-inflammatory prostaglandins that promote tumor growth. Improves outcomes alongside chemotherapy.
supplements.dose: High dose by weight daily

safety_warnings: ALL new skin lumps need fine-needle aspirate test — mast cell tumors look identical to benign lipomas
NEVER squeeze or massage any skin lump — histamine release causes anaphylaxis
Darier's sign (skin wheal and redness when lump is rubbed) is highly suspicious for MCT — tell vet immediately
Surgical excision with wide margins is the primary treatment — early surgery = best prognosis

cause: Prolonged cold or wet exposure without shelter, falling into cold water, anesthesia complications, or illness reducing heat production. Small dogs, puppies, senior dogs, and thin-coated breeds are at extreme risk.
warning: When shivering stops in a still-cold dog, this is a DANGER SIGN — not improvement. The body has run out of energy to shiver. Rush to vet immediately.

medicines.name: IV Warm Fluids (Internal Rewarming — Vet Only)
medicines.dose: Warmed IV saline at 40°C delivered by vet. Fastest and safest internal rewarming method.
medicines.note: External rewarming is slower — always get vet care for temperatures below 33°C.

topical_treatments.name: Warm Water Bottles + Thermal Blanket (Home First Aid)
topical_treatments.active: Warm water bottles wrapped in towels
topical_treatments.use: Place warm (NOT hot) water bottles wrapped in towels in armpits and groin. Fan with warm air. Replace bottles every 15 minutes. Stop active rewarming at 37.5°C to prevent rebound hyperthermia.

supplements.name: NutriCal High-Calorie Recovery Paste
supplements.benefit: Provides rapid high-calorie glucose and fat to fuel the body's heat production during hypothermia recovery. Prevents hypoglycemia that accompanies hypothermia.
supplements.dose: Small amount on finger — allow dog to lick freely if conscious. Do NOT force-feed to unresponsive dog.

safety_warnings: NEVER use electric heating pads directly on skin — hypothermic dogs cannot sense burns
Shivering stopping in a still-cold dog = DANGER SIGN. Rush to vet.
NEVER warm too quickly — rapid rewarming causes dangerous heart arrhythmias
Small dogs, puppies, and senior dogs get hypothermic at temperatures other dogs tolerate — use dog jackets

cause: Common dog toxins: chocolate (theobromine), grapes/raisins (unknown toxin — kidney failure), xylitol (sugar-free gum/peanut butter — liver failure), rat poison (anticoagulant), ibuprofen, paracetamol, onions, macadamia nuts, alcohol.
warning: Do NOT induce vomiting at home without vet instruction — for caustic substances, sharp objects, or certain toxins, vomiting causes more damage. Call ASPCA Poison Control or your vet immediately.

medicines.name: Apomorphine (Induced Vomiting) — Vet Only
medicines.dose: Only if ingestion within 1-2 hours and toxin is not caustic or sharp
medicines.note: Timing is critical — vomiting after 2 hours is ineffective as toxin is already absorbed

medicines.name: Activated Charcoal (Toxin Binding)
medicines.dose: 1-4 g/kg orally by vet after vomiting is controlled
medicines.note: Binds many toxins in the gut before absorption. Not effective for all toxins (e.g., alcohol, xylitol).

supplements.name: SAMe (Denosyl) + Milk Thistle (Liver Protection)
supplements.benefit: For toxins causing liver damage (xylitol, paracetamol, mushrooms). SAMe replenishes glutathione and Milk Thistle regenerates liver cells during recovery.
supplements.dose: Begin under vet guidance after acute phase. Not a substitute for emergency treatment.

safety_warnings: EMERGENCY — call vet or ASPCA Poison Control immediately. Do not wait for symptoms.
NEVER give milk, salt water, or hydrogen peroxide at home without vet instruction
Keep ALL human medications, xylitol products, grapes, chocolate, and onions out of dog reach
Bring the packaging of what the dog ate to the vet — helps identify exact toxin and dose

cause: Weakness of the connective tissue anchoring the tear gland of the third eyelid, allowing it to prolapse and appear as a red mass in the inner corner of the eye. Common in Bulldogs, Beagles, Cocker Spaniels, and Bloodhounds.
warning: Do NOT attempt to push the gland back yourself — this risks permanent damage to the gland. This gland produces 30-40% of the eye's tear film — removing it causes permanent dry eye.

medicines.name: Tobramycin Eye Drops (Prevent secondary infection)
medicines.dose: 1-2 drops in affected eye 3 times daily until surgery
medicines.note: Prevents bacterial infection of the exposed prolapsed gland tissue before surgical correction

topical_treatments.name: Sterile Lubricating Eye Drops (Artificial Tears)
topical_treatments.use: Apply 1-2 drops to keep prolapsed gland moist before surgical correction. Prevents drying and damage to the gland tissue.

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow Omega-3 (Tear Film Support)
supplements.benefit: Supports healthy tear film production and reduces ocular surface inflammation. Important for long-term eye health after cherry eye surgery.

safety_warnings: NEVER remove the cherry eye gland surgically — it produces 30-40% of tear film. Removal causes permanent dry eye.
Surgical repositioning (pocket technique) is the correct procedure — not removal
Without treatment, the exposed gland becomes chronically inflamed and damaged
Both eyes are often affected — even if only one shows cherry eye initially

cause: Accumulation of beta-amyloid plaques in the brain (similar to Alzheimer's in humans) causing progressive cognitive decline. Affects dogs over 10 years old — 50% of dogs over 15 have significant CCD.
warning: CCD is progressive and incurable but highly manageable. Environmental enrichment, diet, and supplements significantly slow progression and maintain quality of life.

medicines.name: Selegiline (Anipryl / Selgian)
medicines.note: Only FDA-approved drug for CCD. Increases dopamine in the brain. Takes 4-6 weeks to see effect.

supplements.name: MCT Oil (Medium Chain Triglycerides — Brain Fuel)
supplements.benefit: Provides alternative ketone fuel source for neurons when glucose metabolism fails in aging brain. Studies show significant cognitive improvement.

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow Omega-3 DHA (Neuroprotective)
supplements.benefit: DHA is the primary structural fat in brain cell membranes.May support cognitive function and brain health and supports neuronal communication.

safety_warnings: CCD is progressive — start supplements and medication EARLY for best effect
Maintain consistent daily routine — changes in schedule significantly worsen confusion
Night wandering and vocalization is common — create a safe confined sleeping area
Rule out pain (arthritis), hearing loss, and vision loss before attributing all symptoms to CCD

cause: Most commonly caused by plaque and tartar buildup on teeth (periodontal disease). Can also be caused by kidney disease (ammonia smell), liver disease (sweet/musty smell), diabetes (fruity smell), or eating feces/garbage.
warning: Very sweet or fruity smelling breath = possible diabetes. Ammonia or urine smell = possible kidney disease. These need immediate vet blood tests.

medicines.name: Clindamycin (only if gum infection confirmed)
medicines.note: Only prescribed if there is active gum infection. Bad breath from tartar needs professional dental cleaning first.

topical_treatments.name: Virbac C.E.T. Enzymatic Dog Toothpaste
topical_treatments.use: Brush teeth daily or minimum 3 times per week. NEVER use human toothpaste — xylitol and fluoride are toxic to dogs.

topical_treatments.name: Tropiclean Fresh Breath Water Additive
topical_treatments.use: Add 1 capful per 250ml of drinking water daily. Tasteless and odorless — dogs accept it easily. Reduces plaque and bacteria.

supplements.name: Probiotic (Purina Fortiflora)
supplements.benefit: Balances oral and gut bacteria that contribute to bad breath. Reduces sulfur-producing bacteria in the mouth.
supplements.dose: 1 sachet daily sprinkled on food

safety_warnings: NEVER use human toothpaste — xylitol is toxic and causes liver failure in dogs
Sweet fruity breath = possible diabetes. Ammonia breath = possible kidney disease. See vet for blood tests.
Professional dental cleaning under anesthesia is needed to remove tartar under the gum line
Start dental care from puppyhood — prevention is far easier than treating advanced disease

cause: Seasonal shedding is normal. Abnormal excessive shedding can be caused by poor nutrition, stress, allergies, parasites (fleas, mites), hormonal imbalance (hypothyroidism, Cushing's), or pregnancy.
warning: If bald patches appear with no regrowth, or shedding is sudden and severe — get a vet blood test to rule out hypothyroidism or Cushing's disease.

medicines.name: No specific medicine for normal shedding
medicines.note: If hypothyroidism or Cushing's is confirmed — specific medication prescribed by vet

topical_treatments.name: Himalaya Erina Coat Conditioner Shampoo
topical_treatments.use: Bathe once a week. Massage well into coat, leave 3 minutes, rinse thoroughly. Strengthens hair follicles and reduces breakage.

topical_treatments.name: Furminator Deshedding Brush
topical_treatments.use: Brush 2-3 times weekly to remove loose undercoat before it falls on furniture. Reduces shedding by up to 90%.

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow Omega-3 (Coat Health)
supplements.benefit: Omega-3 fatty acids strengthen hair follicles, reduce inflammation causing hair fall, and add shine to the coat. Most effective supplement for shedding.

supplements.name: Biotin (Vitamin B7) Supplement
supplements.benefit: Biotin is essential for keratin production — the protein that makes up hair. Reduces breakage and promotes thicker coat regrowth.

safety_warnings: Bald patches with no regrowth = see vet for thyroid and hormone blood tests
Sudden heavy shedding after illness, surgery, or delivery is normal (telogen effluvium) and resolves in 2-3 months
Never shave a double-coated breed (Huskies, Goldens) — destroys the natural insulation and coat may not regrow correctly
Poor diet is the #1 cause of abnormal shedding — switch to high-quality protein-rich food

cause: Low humidity in winter, over-bathing with harsh shampoos, poor diet lacking fatty acids, allergies, hypothyroidism, or parasites (Cheyletiella — walking dandruff mite).
warning: If dandruff is moving (literally looks like it is walking) — this is Cheyletiella mites, a contagious parasite. Needs antiparasitic treatment, not moisturiser.

medicines.name: No specific medicine for simple dry skin
medicines.dose: Focus on diet, supplements, and correct shampoo. Medicated treatment only if underlying cause found.
medicines.note: If Cheyletiella mites confirmed — treat with Fipronil spot-on or ivermectin as directed by vet

topical_treatments.name: Virbac Allermyl Moisturising Shampoo
topical_treatments.use: Use once a week maximum. Leave on 5 minutes before rinsing. Over-bathing worsens dry skin.

topical_treatments.name: Coconut Oil (Topical Moisturiser)
topical_treatments.use: Warm a small amount between palms and massage gently into dry skin patches after bath. Safe if dog licks it.

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow Omega-3 (Skin Hydration)
supplements.benefit: EPA and DHA rebuild the skin's natural oil barrier from inside, reducing water loss through the skin and eliminating flakiness.
supplements.dose: Daily with food. Most effective supplement for dry skin — results in 3-4 weeks.

safety_warnings: Do NOT bathe more than once a week — over-bathing strips natural oils causing more dryness
Moving dandruff (walking dandruff) = Cheyletiella mites, not dry skin — needs antiparasitic treatment
NEVER use human moisturisers or lotions — many contain chemicals toxic to dogs when licked
Add omega-3 to diet before trying expensive shampoos — diet is the root cause in 70% of cases

cause: Bile accumulates in an empty stomach and irritates the lining, causing vomiting of yellow or greenish foam — usually in the early morning before breakfast. Common in dogs fed once daily.
warning: If yellow vomiting happens more than 2-3 times per week, or if the dog also has diarrhea, lethargy, or weight loss — see vet to rule out liver disease, pancreatitis, or inflammatory bowel disease.

medicines.name: Famotidine (Pepcid AC) — OTC
medicines.note: Reduces stomach acid production. Plain Famotidine only — not formulations with other ingredients.

medicines.name: Omeprazole (Prilosec) — OTC
medicines.note: More powerful acid suppression than Famotidine. Give on empty stomach 30 minutes before first meal.

supplements.name: Purina Fortiflora Probiotic
supplements.benefit: Supports healthy gut bacteria and reduces gastric irritation from bile. Helps regulate stomach acid production.
supplements.dose: 1 sachet daily with food

safety_warnings: Simplest fix: give a small snack before bed so the stomach is not completely empty overnight
Switch from once-daily to twice-daily feeding — this alone resolves most cases
Blood or brown coffee-ground material in vomit = emergency vet immediately
If vomiting is frequent — rule out pancreatitis and IBD with vet blood tests before assuming it is just bile

cause: Most commonly early osteoarthritis — joints stiffen during rest and loosen up with movement. Also caused by patellar luxation, hip dysplasia, old injury, or Lyme disease in tick-endemic areas.
warning: If stiffness is sudden, severe, or accompanied by fever and swollen joints — see vet to rule out immune-mediated polyarthritis or Lyme disease.

medicines.name: Meloxicam (Metacam) NSAID
medicines.note: NEVER give ibuprofen, aspirin, or paracetamol — all are toxic and potentially fatal to dogs

topical_treatments.name: Warm Compress on Stiff Joints
topical_treatments.use: Apply warm damp cloth to stiff joints for 10-15 minutes before walks. Improves blood flow and reduces morning stiffness significantly.

supplements.name: Cosequin DS (Glucosamine + Chondroitin + MSM)
supplements.benefit: Rebuilds joint cartilage and lubricates joints. Reduces stiffness over time. Safe for lifelong daily use.
supplements.dose: Double loading dose for first 4-6 weeks. Allow 6-8 weeks before judging effectiveness.

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow Omega-3 (Joint Inflammation)
supplements.benefit: Reduces inflammatory mediators in joint fluid that cause stiffness and pain. Works synergistically with Glucosamine.
supplements.dose: Daily with food long-term

safety_warnings: NEVER give human painkillers — ibuprofen and paracetamol cause fatal kidney failure in dogs
Maintain lean body weight — excess weight is the single biggest aggravating factor for joint stiffness
Short gentle walks are better than long walks for arthritic dogs — little and often
Get an orthopedic memory foam bed — hard floors dramatically worsen stiffness

cause: Common causes: diabetes mellitus, kidney disease, Cushing's disease, pyometra (intact females), liver disease, hypercalcemia, or certain medications (steroids, diuretics). Always needs blood and urine tests to find the cause.
warning: Excessive thirst + urination is NEVER normal. It always indicates an underlying medical condition that must be diagnosed. Do not reduce water intake — the dog needs it.

medicines.name: Depends entirely on underlying diagnosis
medicines.dose: Blood tests + urine tests + ultrasound needed before any medication is prescribed
medicines.note: Do NOT restrict water. The dog drinks more because something is wrong — water is keeping it alive.

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow Omega-3 (Kidney + Organ Support)
supplements.benefit: Safe to give regardless of which organ is affected.

safety_warnings: NEVER restrict water access — the dog needs it and restricting causes serious harm
Always see a vet — excessive thirst is a symptom, not a disease, and always has a treatable underlying cause
Intact female dogs with excessive thirst may have pyometra (infected uterus) — life-threatening emergency
Bring a urine sample to the vet appointment — collect the first morning urine in a clean container

cause: Clear discharge: allergies, excitement, or mild irritation. Yellow or green discharge: bacterial or fungal infection. Blood-tinged discharge: foreign body in nose, nasal tumor, or fungal infection (Aspergillosis). One nostril only: foreign body or tumor.
warning: Blood from only ONE nostril in a middle-aged to older dog = possible nasal tumor or Aspergillus fungal infection. See vet urgently for scoping and imaging.

medicines.name: Doxycycline (bacterial nasal infection)
medicines.note: For confirmed bacterial rhinitis. Culture needed for chronic or non-responding cases.

topical_treatments.name: Sterile Saline Nasal Flush
topical_treatments.use: Gently flush one nostril at a time using a syringe or dropper. Removes debris and mucus.

supplements.name: Vitamin C + Zinc Immune Support
supplements.benefit: Reduces severity and duration of nasal infections.

safety_warnings: Blood from one nostril in an older dog = possible tumor or fungal infection. See vet urgently.
Never use human nasal decongestants (Otrivin, Nasivion) — contain xylometazoline which is toxic to dogs
Brachycephalic breeds (Pugs, Bulldogs) have structurally narrow nostrils — chronic discharge is common but still needs monitoring
Persistent nasal discharge requires veterinary evaluation to determine the underlying cause.

cause: Dehydration (most common), low fiber diet, ingested hair or foreign material, lack of exercise, painful anal glands, enlarged prostate (intact males), or side effect of certain medications.
warning: If dog has not had a bowel movement in more than 48 hours AND is straining hard with no output — see vet. Obstipation (complete blockage) requires enema or manual removal by vet.

medicines.name: Lactulose (Stool Softener)
medicines.note: Safe and gentle. Works by drawing water into the colon to soften stool. Takes 12-24 hours to work.

supplements.name: Psyllium Husk Fiber (Natural Stool Softener)
supplements.benefit: Absorbs water and adds bulk to stool, making it easier to pass. Safe for long-term daily use as dietary fiber.

supplements.name: Purina Fortiflora Probiotic
supplements.benefit: Restores healthy gut bacteria balance which regulates bowel movement frequency and stool consistency.

safety_warnings: NEVER give human laxatives (Dulcolax, Castor oil, Senna) — these cause dangerous cramping and electrolyte loss in dogs
Always increase water intake when treating constipation — dehydration is the most common cause
No bowel movement in 48+ hours with straining = see vet for possible enema
Intact male dogs over 5 years: constipation may indicate enlarged prostate — get checked

cause: Lymph nodes swell in response to: local infection near the node (most common), systemic infection (tick disease, Leptospirosis), immune-mediated disease, or most seriously — lymphoma (cancer of the lymphatic system).
warning: Swollen lymph nodes that are firm, painless, and involve multiple nodes simultaneously = POSSIBLE LYMPHOMA. This is one of the most common cancers in dogs. See vet immediately for fine-needle aspirate.

medicines.name: Diagnosis required before any treatment
medicines.note: Treating presumed infection with antibiotics when it is actually lymphoma wastes critical treatment time
